trial_data.os: Median OS 30 months (once-daily RT) and 25 months (twice-daily RT)

tier_1_regimens.notes: Four cycles recommended; cycle length every 21-28 days during concurrent RT. Myeloid growth factors not recommended during concurrent chemoRT (category 1 for not using GM-CSF). Originally category 1, downgraded to category 2A in v1.2027. Reference [2] in algorithm.

trial_data.os: Median OS 23 months (BID RT) vs 19 months (daily RT)

tier_1_regimens.notes: Preferred regimen; four cycles. Cycle length every 21-28 days with concurrent RT. Originally category 1, changed to category 2A in v1.2027. Reference [3] in algorithm.
Used if cisplatin contraindicated or not tolerated. Four cycles; cycle length every 21-28 days. Preferred regimen based on subgroup analysis of ADRIATIC trial (Cheng et al. 2024) [4].

drugs.name: Durvalumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1 every 28 days for up to 24 months

trial_data.os: Median OS 55.9 months in durvalumab arm vs 33.4 months (HR 0.73, p=0.01)

tier_1_regimens.notes: For patients with stage I-III LS-SCLC, PS 0-1, no progression after chemoradiation. Continue until progression or intolerable toxicity, or maximum 24 months. If PCI considered, give before durvalumab. Imaging every 8 weeks for first 18 months, then every 12 weeks. Reference [5] in algorithm.

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Cisplatin 25 mg/m2 Days 1,2,3 + Etoposide 100 mg/m2 Days 1,2,3
tier_1_regimens.notes: Alternative regimen for LS-SCLC. Four cycles. Reference [2] in algorithm.

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Etoposide 100 mg/m2 + Atezolizumab 1200 mg (induction) → Atezolizumab 1200 mg maintenance

drugs.name: Atezolizumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1 every 21 days x 4 cycles (induction), then maintenance 1200 mg Day 1 every 21 days

trial_data.os: Median OS 12.3 months vs 10.3 months (HR 0.76; p=0.0154); 1-year OS 51.9% vs 39.0%

tier_1_regimens.notes: Four cycles induction, then continue atezolizumab until progression or toxicity. Atezolizumab and hyaluronidase-tqjs subcutaneous injection may substitute. Contraindications: active autoimmune disease, immunosuppressive agents. For patients with asymptomatic untreated brain metastases included in CASPIAN but not IMpower133. Reference [6] in algorithm.

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Etoposide 100 mg/m2 + Atezolizumab 1200 mg (induction) → Atezolizumab 1680 mg maintenance

drugs.name: Atezolizumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1 every 21 days x 4 cycles (induction), then maintenance Atezolizumab 1680 mg Day 1 every 28 days

tier_1_regimens.notes: Alternative maintenance dosing; category 1 based on FDA approval. Same efficacy and safety profile. Note that category 1 recommendation is specifically for the 1200 mg maintenance dose used in the trial; however, 1680 mg every 28 days is an approved alternative. Reference [6].

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Etoposide 100 mg/m2 + Atezolizumab 1200 mg (induction) → Lurbinectedin 3.2 mg/m2 + Atezolizumab 1200 mg maintenance

drugs.name: Atezolizumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1 every 21 days x 4 cycles (induction), then maintenance Atezolizumab 1200 mg Day 1 every 21 days

drugs.name: Lurbinectedin
drugs.schedule: Day 1 every 21 days (starting after induction, in maintenance)

tier_1_regimens.notes: For ES-SCLC patients with ECOG PS 0-1, no history of brain metastases, and at least stable disease after 4 cycles induction chemoimmunotherapy. Not for re-treatment if lurbinectedin used previously. Lurbinectedin added to atezolizumab maintenance. Reference [7] in algorithm.

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Carboplatin AUC 5-6 + Etoposide 80-100 mg/m2 + Durvalumab 1500 mg (induction) → Durvalumab 1500 mg maintenance

drugs.name: Durvalumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1 every 21 days x 4 cycles (induction), then maintenance Durvalumab 1500 mg Day 1 every 28 days

trial_data.os: Median OS 13.0 months vs 10.3 months (HR 0.73; 95% CI 0.59-0.91; p=0.0047); 1-year OS 52.8% vs 39.3%

tier_1_regimens.notes: Includes patients with asymptomatic untreated brain metastases. Four cycles induction then maintenance durvalumab until progression or toxicity. Carboplatin preferred over cisplatin in CASPIAN (78% received carboplatin). Reference [8] in algorithm.

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Cisplatin 75-80 mg/m2 + Etoposide 80-100 mg/m2 + Durvalumab 1500 mg (induction) → Durvalumab 1500 mg maintenance

drugs.name: Durvalumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1 every 21 days x 4 cycles (induction), then maintenance Durvalumab 1500 mg Day 1 every 28 days

tier_1_regimens.notes: Alternative to carboplatin-based regimen. Included in CASPIAN trial. Four cycles induction then maintenance. Reference [8].
Other recommended regimen for ES-SCLC; 4-6 cycles based on response and tolerability. Reference [9] in algorithm.
Other recommended regimen for ES-SCLC. Reference [10].
Other recommended regimen. Reference [11].

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Cisplatin 25 mg/m2 Days 1,2,3 + Etoposide 100 mg/m2 Days 1,2,3 (no immunotherapy)
tier_1_regimens.notes: Other recommended regimen. Reference [12].
Useful in certain circumstances for ES-SCLC. Reference [13].
Useful in certain circumstances. Reference [14].

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Cisplatin 30 mg/m2 Days 1,8 + Irinotecan 65 mg/m2 Days 1,8
tier_1_regimens.notes: Useful in certain circumstances. Reference [15].

drugs.name: Tarlatamab-dlle
drugs.schedule: Dosing schedule not specified in algorithm; from DeLLphi trials, typically every 2 weeks

trial_data.os: Median OS 13.6 months vs 8.3 months; 6-month OS 76% vs 62%; 12-month OS 53% vs 37%

tier_1_regimens.notes: For extensive-stage SCLC with disease progression on or after platinum-based chemotherapy. Category 1. Manage cytokine release syndrome per NCCN Guidelines for CAR T-Cell and Lymphocyte Engager-Related Toxicities. Reference [16] in algorithm.
Consider for patients with CNS disease. Reference [17,18].

trial_data.os: Median OS 16.2 months (in subset of patients eligible for platinum rechallenge)

tier_1_regimens.notes: Preferred if not previously used. FDA approved. Reference [19,20].
If prolonged disease-free time (previously classified as sensitive relapse), rechallenge with original platinum/etoposide regimen with or without immunotherapy. References [21-25].

trial_data.trial_name: von Pawel et al. 1999; O'Brien et al. 2006
trial_data.os: Median OS 25 weeks (IV) vs 24.7 weeks (CAV); oral topotecan improved OS vs BSC (26 vs 14 weeks)

tier_1_regimens.notes: Both oral and IV are acceptable. Attenuated dose may be used; excessive toxicity at 1.5 mg/m2 IV x5 days. References [17,23,26-28].
Other recommended subsequent therapy. Reference [26].
Other recommended subsequent therapy. Reference [29].

trial_data.trial_name: Masters et al. 2003; van der Lee et al. 2001
trial_data.os: Median survival 7.1 months (Masters); 17 weeks (van der Lee)

tier_1_regimens.notes: Other recommended subsequent therapy. References [30-32].

Other recommended for patients not previously treated with ICI. Subcutaneous injection (nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy) may substitute. Avoid if disease progressed on prior ICI (atezolizumab/durvalumab). FDA indication withdrawn but considered effective. References [33-36].

Other recommended for patients not previously treated with ICI. Subcutaneous injection (pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph) may substitute. Avoid if disease progressed on prior ICI. FDA indication withdrawn. References [33-36].

trial_data.trial_name: Johnson et al. 1990; Einhorn et al. 1990

tier_1_regimens.notes: Other recommended subsequent therapy. References [37,38].

trial_data.trial_name: Smit et al. 1998; Yamamoto et al. 2006

tier_1_regimens.notes: Other recommended subsequent therapy. References [39,40].

trial_data.trial_name: Pietanza et al. 2012; Zauderer et al. 2014
trial_data.orr: 23% in sensitive SCLC; higher in MGMT-methylated tumors

tier_1_regimens.notes: Other recommended subsequent therapy; may be useful for brain metastases. References [40-42].

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Systemic cytotoxic chemotherapy (per SCLC guidelines) for transformed SCLC
tier_1_regimens.site: Transformed SCLC from NSCLC with oncogenic driver

biomarkers.requirement: Histologically confirmed transformed SCLC after TKI therapy

tier_1_regimens.notes: Rare population. Cytotoxic chemotherapy regimens from SCLC guidelines are recommended. Immunotherapy role unclear; avoid if TKI continued due to known toxicity. Consider referral to center with expertise. References [43-48].
